title: Error: short-name resolution enforced but cannot prompt without a TTY
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**BUG REPORT**

/kind bug


**Description**

When installing rootless kind, using:
```
KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_PROVIDER=podman kind create cluster --name test
```

getting an error:
```
command "podman pull kindest/node@sha256:69860bda5563ac81e3c0057d654b5253219618a22ec3a346306239bba8cfa1a6" failed with error: exit status 125
Command Output: Error: short-name resolution enforced but cannot prompt without a TTY
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. go get sigs.k8s.io/kind@v0.11.1 

2. KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_PROVIDER=podman kind create cluster --name test


**Describe the results you received:**

Error: short-name resolution enforced but cannot prompt without a TTY

**Describe the results you expected:**

No errors

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

```
[tomas@uola ~]$ KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_PROVIDER=podman kind create cluster --name test
using podman due to KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_PROVIDER
enabling experimental podman provider
Cgroup controller detection is not implemented for Podman. If you see cgroup-related errors, you might need to set systemd property "Delegate=yes", see https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/rootless/
Creating cluster "test" ...
 ✗ Ensuring node image (kindest/node:v1.21.1) 🖼 
ERROR: failed to create cluster: failed to pull image "kindest/node@sha256:69860bda5563ac81e3c0057d654b5253219618a22ec3a346306239bba8cfa1a6": command "podman pull kindest/node@sha256:69860bda5563ac81e3c0057d654b5253219618a22ec3a346306239bba8cfa1a6" failed with error: exit status 125
Command Output: Error: short-name resolution enforced but cannot prompt without a TTY

```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.0
API Version:  3.3.0
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Sat Aug 21 05:36:14 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body:
Manually ran podman pull:
```
[tomas@uola ~]$ podman pull kindest/node@sha256:69860bda5563ac81e3c0057d654b5253219618a22ec3a346306239bba8cfa1a6
✔ docker.io/kindest/node@sha256:69860bda5563ac81e3c0057d654b5253219618a22ec3a346306239bba8cfa1a6
Trying to pull docker.io/kindest/node@sha256:69860bda5563ac81e3c0057d654b5253219618a22ec3a346306239bba8cfa1a6...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 92397077056a done  
Copying blob 2c8dd3a52776 done  
Copying blob 9178282edeae done  
Copying blob b8ff34c1005f done  
Copying blob 6415519001ad done  
Copying blob 259d51f13710 done  
Copying blob 6d2c2164c4af done  
Copying blob 5e79b1e14903 done  
Copying blob da636a2fd1fa done  
Copying blob d69368c3d04e done  
Copying blob 41e3227605a3 done  
Copying blob b68a62b92963 done  
Copying config 32b8b755de done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
32b8b755dee8d5fc6dbafef80898e7f2e450655f45f4b59cca3f7f57e9278c3b
```
After which kind create cluster went ok
```
[tomas@uola ~]$ KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_PROVIDER=podman kind create cluster --name test
using podman due to KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_PROVIDER
enabling experimental podman provider
Cgroup controller detection is not implemented for Podman. If you see cgroup-related errors, you might need to set systemd property "Delegate=yes", see https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/rootless/
Creating cluster "test" ...
 ✓ Ensuring node image (kindest/node:v1.21.1) 🖼 
 ✓ Preparing nodes 📦  
 ✓ Writing configuration 📜 
 ✓ Starting control-plane 🕹️ 
 ✓ Installing CNI 🔌 
 ✓ Installing StorageClass 💾 
Set kubectl context to "kind-test"
You can now use your cluster with:

kubectl cluster-info --context kind-test

Have a question, bug, or feature request? Let us know! https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/#community 🙂
```

They would eventually have to start using long names: 

https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/container-image-short-names

Docker does this too, but is a bit more secretive about it.

```
Status: Image is up to date for kindest/node@sha256:69860bda5563ac81e3c0057d654b5253219618a22ec3a346306239bba8cfa1a6
docker.io/kindest/node@sha256:69860bda5563ac81e3c0057d654b5253219618a22ec3a346306239bba8cfa1a6
```


~~Did you file a bug with [kind](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kind) already ?~~

The workaround is disabling strict mode...

Seems at least related to https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kind/issues/2186

They need to use `docker.io/kindest/node` for podman

This does look like intended behavior. The default mode for shortname handling in F34 is enforcing, and if we can't prompt (typically, if we're being invoked by a script, like kind), we hard-fail. This is necessary for security reasons - details can be found [here](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/container-image-short-names).

I concur with @afbjorklund that this is related to the linked kind issue, and is fixed there. Recommend you update to a more recent kind; if that fails, please raise an issue upstream with them. Closing as such.

I had a similar problem on the windows command prompt.

When I run this, I get the same error message as you:

`podman run -e 'MESSAGE_WELCOME=Hello, world DIT' test/hello-world`

When I run this instead, I get no error message and all works as intended::

`podman run -e "MESSAGE_WELCOME=Hello, world DIT" test/hello-world`

(Take a close look at the quotes around `MESSAGE_WELCOME`.)

I hope that helps somehow.

On `cmd.exe` on Windows, I have the same issue independent of the quotes.

Installed Podman 4.3.0 using `podman-desktop`.

> On `cmd.exe` on Windows, I have the same issue independent of the quotes.
> 
> Installed Podman 4.3.0 using `podman-desktop`.

What image are you trying to run?
I had the same problem running `datalust/seq`. If I prefixed the container image with the registry name, it worked

e.g. 

`podman run docker.io/datalust/seq`

Hope that helps :-)

Specify the full name:

`podman pull docker.io/postgrest/postgrest`
Is there a way to disable short name resolution enforcement? Also, for podman, is there another file to change what server will be used with a short name?

> Is there a way to disable short name resolution enforcement? Also, for podman, is there another file to change what server will be used with a short name?

The [containers-registries.conf man page ](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-registries.conf.5.md#short-name-aliasing-modes)goes into the details.
